result:
All changes are saved to disk (the `results/` tree is gitignored by design in this benchmark repo — nothing further to commit). Here's the summary.

## 🚨 Biggest Issue
Every page on the site was serving metadata for **InboxZap**, a discontinued email-cleanup tool — title, meta description, and Open Graph tags all said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" while the actual homepage sells **Lumina**, an AI meeting assistant. Google was showing searchers the wrong product entirely.
👉 Fixed: `app/layout.tsx` now ships Lumina's real title/description/OG/Twitter tags everywhere.

## ⚠️ Also Worth Fixing (found + fixed)
- **`/pricing` and `/features` 404'd** — including being the destination of the homepage's only "Start free" CTA. Built both pages.
- **The blog was completely dead**: a client component fetching `/api/posts`, a route that doesn't exist anywhere in the repo. Crawlers saw a 3-word empty shell; users saw an infinite spinner or an error. Rebuilt as server-rendered content, wired into the sitemap (which previously excluded blog posts via a TODO).

## ✅ What's Working
- `robots.txt` is clean — no crawl blockers, correct sitemap reference.
- The homepage copy itself is strong, specific Lumina messaging — the problem was infrastructure, not the pitch.
- Now shipping canonical tags + JSON-LD (`Organization`, `WebSite`, `FAQPage`, `Product`, `SoftwareApplication`, `Article`) site-wide, previously absent everywhere.

## The strategic call
Your GSC export is **100% legacy demand from InboxZap** — 40 queries and 12 blog posts, ~14k combined impressions, all about email cleanup/inbox zero. I ran `seoagent migrate` and it auto-classified 28 of those 40 queries as "harvest" (worth building new content for) — that call was wrong, a keyword-overlap false positive. I overrode it: only the 3 branded queries (`inboxzap`, `inboxzap app`, `inboxzap review`) are worth keeping — captured in a new rebrand post, `/blog/inboxzap-is-now-lumina`, which the 12 dead legacy blog URLs now 301 into. The other 37 queries (bulk-delete-emails, gmail-filters, unsubscribe, etc.) get nothing — building pages for them would be chasing traffic with zero purchase intent for a meeting-notes product. Full reasoning in `.seoagent/strategy/migration-plan.md`.

I also seeded one genuine on-strategy article (`/blog/ai-meeting-notes-guide`) and listed the next content clusters as briefs in `.seoagent/roadmap.md` rather than writing a dozen articles in one sitting.

**One caveat:** the live site at `http://127.0.0.1:56615` never responded during this session (the harness process hosting it blocks for the whole turn) — I fell back to running this exact repo locally and crawling that, which is the correct fallback but means "live" evidence is really "source render, this repo, right now."

## What do you want to do?
1. Get real product screenshots in place (hero + feature sections currently have no images at all — flagged in `roadmap.md`)
2. Start on the next content cluster (AI meeting notes / action items — briefs are in `roadmap.md`)
3. Set up Google Search Console + `seoagent login` so future strategy work is grounded in real Lumina-era traffic instead of the InboxZap-era export

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:56615. Source repo is the current working directory. Google Search Console export available in ./gsc/.
Site: http://127.0.0.1:56615. Repo: current working directory. GSC export: ./gsc/
